fixture_meta.client_status: Real, ongoing client project -- first fully in-house (from-scratch) Stirg design, not a subcontractor-transferred BOM like Stadler/GST.
fixture_meta.sibling_fixture: winkler_900l_structured.json (91 part numbers shared between both -- see findings_for_cross_analysis)
source_files: Offer_Winkler.xlsx (manual procurement/quote spreadsheet, shared across both tanks)
fixture_meta.private_payload: kb-private-fixtures/STIRG/winkler/suppliers.json -- 93 supplier contact records (PII), not embedded here, never pushed to public repo

columns_raw_to_key.Item: item (Inventor hierarchy path, dot-separated; gaps from CAD edits are normal, not meaningful)
columns_raw_to_key.Part Number: part_number (raw, as typed in Inventor -- for self-designed parts this is a structured code 'NNN.AXX.SXX.[PT]NNN' optionally with a description appended directly after with no fixed separator (seen: '-', '_', ' ', or none); for purchased parts this is a free-text manufacturer/description string with no structured code)
columns_raw_to_key.Thumbnail: embedded JPEG, extracted separately -- see image_storage
columns_raw_to_key.BOM Structure: bom_structure -- Inventor-native field (Normal/Purchased/Inseparable seen). NOT a reliable make/buy signal: most actually-purchased items (Kohler valves, JUMO sensor, Harting connector) are tagged 'Normal'; only FLEXA conduit + KVT fasteners are tagged 'Purchased'.
columns_raw_to_key.Thickness: thickness_mm_raw -- only populated for flat/sheet-metal parts (25/143 in 200L, 26/152 in 900L), European comma-decimal mm string as typed (e.g. '0,500 mm')
columns_raw_to_key.Material: material_raw -- heterogeneous formatting preserved as-is (e.g. 'EN 1.4301 AISI 304' vs bare '1.4571' vs 'Generic' placeholder for untracked purchased/standard parts)

schema_notes.no_mass_column: Unlike Stadler/GST Inventor exports, this export has no mass/weight column at all -- costing in Offer_Winkler.xlsx is driven entirely by manual pricing entry, not weight x rate.
schema_notes.no_operation_status_columns: Unlike GST's Inventor export, this BOM has zero operation/status columns (no cad_fixed/laser_cut/etc analog) -- manufacturing status tracking is not present in CAD metadata for this source at all; would come entirely from PPM's own org_operations model.
schema_notes.part_number_pn_collision_finding: The 'one PN per document' simplification mostly holds, but is not airtight: at least two structured codes are reused for two physically different sibling parts within the SAME tank's BOM -- '001.A01.S02.P002' is used for both '-Bottom Sheet' (200L item 9.4.1.1) and '-Tube1-2 zoll' (200L item 9.4.1.7.5); '001.A00.S01.P001' is used for both '-Handle' (200L item 18.1) and '-Side Cover' (200L item 18.2). These are genuine code collisions, not just description-suffix noise -- held as a finding, not silently resolved.
schema_notes.is_standard_hardware_heuristic: Heuristic only (regex for DIN/ISO/M<n>x<n> patterns in part_number) -- not authoritative, model judgment not separately applied per-row in this pass.

offer_cross_reference.summary: 27 of 807 allocated rows in Offer_Winkler.xlsx's main sheet are populated. 18/27 resolved to a BOM part number via normalized prefix-match (Offer's Artikel-Nr is the bare structured code; BOM's Part Number is code+description concatenated with an inconsistent separator). 9 unresolved.

production_context.fx_rate_note: Matches existing default D-24, confirmed here from a real offer rather than assumed.

field_status_report.status_value_style: Free-text Serbian status phrases (not binary Done/blank like GST, not Stadler's structure either) -- e.g. 'Stiglo' (arrived), 'Nije jos stiglo' (not yet arrived), 'Kod Enel-a oni ugradjuju u sklop' (at Enel's, being installed into the assembly), 'Stiglo, stoji kod Sidek-a u magacinu' (arrived, sitting in Sidek's warehouse), 'Poslato <date>' (sent on date). No contact PII in this sheet -- safe for public fixture, unlike Suppliers.

image_storage.note: Shared single 'winkler' folder across both tanks (not split per-tank) -- 91 part numbers are identical between tanks so this avoids uploading the same canonical thumbnail twice. Deviates from the strict per-source-label-per-fixture path convention used for Stadler/GST; flagged here for visibility.

findings_for_cross_analysis: Hierarchy depth reaches 5 levels in both tanks (e.g. 200L item 9.4.1.6.5, 900L item 54.7.2.4) -- third confirming source against D-96's documented 3-level max, alongside GST (depth 7, OQ-59) and Stadler (OQ-62). Holding per existing convention, not resolving per-source.

91 of ~140-149 unique part numbers are literally shared between the 200L and 900L tanks (identical part, identical thumbnail) -- including a fully identical 'Elektrical Cabinet Box' sub-assembly tree reused verbatim across both products. Real shared-module evidence in self-designed work, same pattern as Stadler's SWC/UWC shared-fittings finding (OQ-62 #6).

Suppliers sheet has exactly 93 records -- identical count to Offer_GST.xlsx's Suppliers sheet. Strongly suggests one master supplier list is being copied across different client offers rather than maintained per-client. Reinforces OQ-59's existing finding that a real supplier master table is a schema gap, now with a second confirming source.

BOM Structure field ('Normal'/'Purchased'/'Inseparable') is not a reliable make/buy signal in this source -- most actually-purchased items are tagged 'Normal'. Offer's own 'Make or Buy' column is also mostly empty (3/27 filled). Neither source currently gives reliable make/buy classification for most parts.
Several leftover unrenamed default Inventor part names appear identically in both tanks ('Part100', 'Part258', 'A$Cc5f8cb19') -- never renamed to a real part number or description.

Genuine part-number code collisions found within a single tank's own BOM (see schema_notes.part_number_pn_collision_finding) -- the 'one PN, no cross-reference needed' simplification is mostly true but not airtight.
